name: demo_visisonor_room
_doc: ITEM-10 DEMO (visi-sonor near-term anchor) — the small 3D room the light show plays in, authored as a NODE ARRANGEMENT (room-is-an-arrangement). NO projector (color/brightness/timing only); a TV/SCREEN with NO projection mapping. It composes ONLY already-merged primitives: Environment (iterable sky) + AssetImport (shell + lamp GLBs, placeholder-safe) + TextureApply (ambientCG CC0 wall/floor) + 3 Light fixtures each with a device addr + 1 LedStrip (per-pixel-addressable) + 1 Screen quad running the classic spectrum-bars visualization off the SAME set_input_frame band seam the lights read. The shell + fixtures compose into ONE scene_node via the Group; the Screen is a standalone node whose `screen` descriptor the demo controller reads + mounts (Group skips non-scene_node children by design). The AUDIO -> band -> freq_to_color -> device.set_led wiring is driven per-frame by the demo controller (aperture/demo_interactions.gd), which injects the live analyzer bands into set_input_frame; this room supplies the drivable fixtures + the screen. Each fixture is drivable by BOTH the 3D renderer (its Light descriptor) AND a real WLED strip (its addr via device.set_led) — one descriptor, any sink. Shell/lamp GLBs are absent until fetched; AssetImport falls back to placeholder box/cylinder meshes so this renders + self-tests headless with zero network fetch (C-ideal). Builds ON demo_room.json (Slice 1C) by ADDING the screen; it is a new arrangement, not an edit to a primitive (N-ideal).
